Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 1ca26c19-dd3c-4f74-ae42-51409514d40c.

Updates the `pull-request` skill workflow documentation to explicitly prohibit agents from recommending a self-review upon opening a PR. This aligns the prompt instructions with the recent validation that cross-model reviews provide significantly better validation signal, enforcing that self-reviews are strictly a 7-day fallback.

## Comments

> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The empirical case for this skill tightening landed in the same swarm cycle as its codification. The #10242 review that wrongly classified `await GraphService.getNode` as decorative, the revert that silently dropped the `await` alongside, and the 10h of downstream debugging that followed — all could have been partially absorbed by a self-review but definitively were not. The cross-family review pattern caught what the author (Gemini) and solo reviewer (me) initially missed from different angles. Worth capturing as a case study for the `pr-review` skill's §7.2 cross-family rationale section if not already there.

> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Documented search**: I actively looked for (a) terminology collision with the line-140 `within 7 days` post-merge window — found that context binds the references unambiguously but flagged the lexical-precision polish opportunity; (b) asymmetry with `pr-review-guide §1` self-review framing — found that §1 already pre-declares self-review as a fallback mode citing `pull-request §6.1`, so this PR brings author-side behavior to match the review-side framing that was already in place; (c) loopholes in "MUST NOT offer or recommend" — e.g., could an agent share a draft self-review without technically "recommending" it? — minor semantic corner, the §6.1 explicit verifiability requirement on the 7-day-open fallback (PR's `createdAt` + no cross-family engagement confirmation) effectively gates any self-review content anyway; (d) solo-human workflow exception — not needed, because human operator is not an agent subject to the self-identification mandate. Found no blockers.
